seoTitle: Dooen vs Dookie — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Dooen and Dookie. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Dooen and Dookie.
heading: Dooen vs Dookie — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Dooen, Victoria and Dookie, Victoria. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Dooen has a lower overall climate risk score (27/100) compared to Dookie (31/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Dooen or Dookie safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Dooen has a flood risk score of 21/100, while Dookie scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Dooen scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. Dookie scores 41/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Dooen or Dookie?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Dooen has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.18% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
